summary_strategy: Reframe summary to emphasize platform engineering ownership, operational reliability, and AI-augmented security delivery—mirroring OpenAI’s FDE tone of pragmatic builders who ship secure systems at scale
skills_strategy: Use condensed 5-line format to maximize relevance; lead with Security Engineering & Automation, Cloud & DevSecOps, and Security Data & AI to align with JD priorities; merge overlapping categories (e.g., API integrations into platform automation) and drop testing/quality automation to reduce noise

experience_focus.must_highlight: operational reliability ownership, cross-functional IAM/security automation, AI-augmented security tooling
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no invented metrics, no speculative ownership (e.g., 'led' only where evidence confirms leadership), no unverified tool names beyond those in source (e.g., avoid 'Terraform' unless implied by IaC)

risk_controls: Replace all 'led', 'pioneered', 'engineered' with 'built', 'developed', or 'delivered' unless evidence confirms leadership; avoid 'architected' unless explicitly stated
Never add metrics beyond those in source—e.g., keep '25 percent' only if tied to KnowBe4 analysis, do not generalize
Remove all corporate filler like 'end-to-end', 'holistic', or 'synergy'; prefer concrete verbs: built, integrated, automated, documented
Do not invent tools (e.g., no 'Terraform' unless implied by IaC), frameworks, or responsibilities beyond resume text
